reviews.review_msg: Cares a lot about students
Cutie patootie
Amazing professor, very sweet and really cares about his students. His classes do require you to read a lot but they're very interesting and you should definitely take ARCH100 or 131 as an elective.
Amazing professor, always willing to help students. The slides provided are enough for the exams if you listen to him during the class as he gives hints about the questions he may ask in the exam.

Amazing teacher he teaches so well and has a lot of interest in students its so easy to pass if you know what do lets be real if you dont pass this you are the most inocent thing in the world or have no interest in the class
Such an amazing professor, One of the best in the Arch department. He truly cares about his students and his lectures are very interesting as he covers in-depth analysis on almost all topics.
Okaish prof. Rejects Graham Hancock's theory of history and Archeology.
Probably the most interesting class at SFU so far. He is funny, has creative explanations, and is engaging. Get ready to memorize stuff on his slides. I wished the class would never end :((
Lovely prof. He is extremely nice and caring. His innocence and friendly nature with students go hand in hand. Will grade you fairly. Super nice much recommended
Amazing guy. He is so innocent that you would want to work and get good grades to not disappoint him. You will also probably cry in your last class with him, he's that nice. DEFINITELY RECOMMENDED!

nice prof, but his lectures are long. His slides are unclear and often lack information/context, so its best to be present for lecture in order to understand. His exams are pretty easy & are mainly based on the lecture slides. The textbook wasn't really necessary, but was helpful to provide insight on the topics he only briefly covers during class

He is one of the nicest instructors I've had and although I took his course as an elective and I'm not a big fan of archaeology, I enjoyed his classes a lot. His exams are mostly based on the class lectures, just a lot of memorization. Try to participate as much as you can and be sure to review his slides and class notes before the exams.

The prof himself was okay, he was friendly and tried to make a boring course more fun. Course is graded on a curve or something, because the syllabus doesn't say what percentage gives you what letter grade. 3 exams determine your grade, but there's no homework and the questions are mostly MC. Textbook was useless, don't buy it.
Exams are easy if you go to lecture. His exams are based on lectures and slides. Lectures are quite boring but he attempts to make it interesting by making jokes.

Ernest himself is a fine prof, nice guy and pretty funny. However the course itself is absolutely brutal. It is the most boring course I have ever taken. There is so much memorization required. This course was not a GPA booster for me and many other people I know who took it. Unless greatly interested in prehistory, do not take this course.

This class was the worst experience. Do not recommend taking it as a GPA booster unless you're good at memorizing thousands of words and could remember weird and new ones too. No complaints towards the professor himself but the course is quite boring & he has way too many slides filled with fluff rather than important info that will be on the test
The best prof I have ever seen before

Class is easy however, make sure you study the slides a LOT. Exams are lot easier compared to the lecture slides. Study each lecture in detail couple of times nd become familiar with the key concepts. It is a straight forward class as long as u study in detail. NOT A EASY CLASS compared to other GPA booster classes u gotta work for the grade.

Took ARCH 100 summer intersession with this professor and it was the worst experiences of my uni career. A lot of slides to memorize and none of the slides made sense as they were put together pretty clumsily. Class average was usually around 65.2%. NOT RECOMMENDED TOUGH PROF

Taken 100, and 252 with him and can tell you with 100% confidence, don't buy the textbooks for these classes. Going off the lecture slides will guarantee a B or higher depending on how much you retain. Go to class and take notes of the things he talks about in detail. The exams are 50 MC and 1 short answer question. Reviews slides a day before.

Do not take as a GPA Booster, Midterms and Final were very hard, all multiple choice but very confusing I didn't do the textbook readings and I still got by just studying the lectures which were 100 slides per week  Just don't do it,

Do not take this course if you are looking for a GPA booster. This class covers so much information and it is difficult to memorize all of it. Lectures can get very boring as well. This course will ruin your GPA if you can't memorize 1000+ slides and the textbook.
exams basically cover everything, do not take this course with this professor IF you want a gpa booster

Nice prof but the class is very boring. Would not recommend this course unless you are interested in archaeology. Exams are difficult as it covers lots of information. The average for the first midterm was 63%. Definitely not a GPA booster.

He seems like a nice prof but his slides are kind of misleading.. he'll have things bolded in red that you think he would test you on but he doesn't. He'll test you on material written in black right down to the smallest detail. This class is probably only a GPA booster with a different instructor.

Ernest Bumann, in my opinion, is the BEST lecturer at SFU. Hands down. He gives solid lectures, is very knowledgeable about the subject and is absolutely hilarious! His lecture notes are all you'll need to do amazing on the exams. Rest assured, this will be your favourite course simply due to Bumann's enthusiastic teaching style.

Great course. Sort of felt unprepared for essay questions, due to large amounts of material. but other than that, great prof. :) LOTS of information though. :( The class was a lot, but the prof made it better :)

Great class to take if you're interested in Ancient Egypt as Ernest is a knowledgable and entertaining lecturer. 6 hours a week (intersession) is a lot but he shows movies to help pass the time. Minimal work as there are 3 tests and no assignments. Study the lecture slides and you'll get a good grade. Textbooks provides extra context but not needed
This guy is a legend. Easy class and is super fun. Tests were all from lecture slides and if you can memorize a little bit, you'll for sure get over 90.

Two hr lecture followed by one hr movie. Attending class is a must and staying for the movie is a must (he has test questions from the movies). Prepare to write everything down, including things he says in verbatim as he will test you on material that is not from the slides. Never used books. 1st MT avg 80%. 2nd MT avg 67%. Final avg 75%.
Nice prof
Going to lectures is your best bet for getting a good grade. Ernest goes into extended detail about what's on his slides, so show up for extra information.
Ernest is a genuinely nice guy and is always willing to talk to students. Much respect for him.
Long lectures, but attending them will help prepare you for exams.
Attend class and go through the notes before exams. You will do well.
Great prof, great lectures.
Long lectures, but fairly simple material. Would take again.
Great lectures, great subject. Overall 1010 class.
Funny guy that knows how to keep the students engaged.
Great lectures, but lots of material to memorize.
Great class to learn some history on Egypt.
great guy and fun class
Ernest is a great lecturer and really knows his stuff!
Great lectures, interesting material.
Long lectures, but very fair exams. Memorize the notes, textbook is not needed.
Ernest is always willing to help you out and doesn't shy away from questions. Show up to class, do the work, and you will get a good grade. Highly recommend.
Lectures are long, but if you put in the effort and show up to class you will do well.
Ernest is one of the best profs at SFU. Truly enjoys teaching his subject and knows a lot about it.
Ernest is a funny guy. Lectures are long but he keeps them light with jokes and quips. Definitely would take another course with him.

Ernest has an extensive knowledge of Ancient Egypt and uses this to make the course interesting for students. The way he teaches allows for maximum knowledge transmission and he provides many interesting analogies that help students remember specific details.
Ernest is an OG best prof in SFU by far. Great guy and makes the class interesting.
super easy! If you go over the power points and key terms and show up, you'll get an A!!

Occasionally funny, but generally long lectures taken from text and mildly condensed. He reads off the slides and rarely adds anything extra. Easy tests if you study, will need to know names and rough dates, dynasties at least. We had a small class so some class discussion.

He teaches at KPU and UBC as well, very easy marker.. 2 people missed a test and he let them re do it without ask any questions on why they missed the test. I never studied and got around 82% on each test. Hes a very nice guy and funny. Has an accent but you can easily understand him.
Easy
Long lectures, but provides fair tests for his students.
Wonderful Prof. Easy tests, if you do the readings and show up you will be fine. Nice guy, highly recommend.
Worst class ever, i don't understand a word he is saying and his lecture are just boring as hell... For the exam, make sure you read all the ppt and chapter, because he basically ask EVERYTHING

honestly, this guy is the best prof in the world. the people who are complaining about his exams being hard are probably the ones that never showed up because there was literally nothing that wasn't covered in class. he's amazing and if i could take any other classes with him i would. other profs should learn from this guy. hes simply wonderful.
as other said he is nice as a person but the subject its self is so boring and alot alot alot of information. I didn't enjoy the subject

he is a kind and fun person. However, he do nothing rather than giving lecture, the TA do everything. The exam is HARD.too much information. If you want to take Arch100 because you want a high or good mark. it's not the right course. I'm a B student( I never get D or F) BUT I got the first F in my life in this subject :( really stay away from arch

Ernest is an incredibly friendly guy and does a fantastic job at explaining things. If you're taking Arch 100, be prepared for a lot of information. This class covers the whole world over thousands of years. It's not easy remembering everything, but he emphasizes important points and tells you everything you need to know.

he is a nice person.many people told me ARCH is an easy A but found out that this one of the worst class I ever take. so boring, so much to remeber, each lectures almost60-70 slides. you have to remebre all the useless information.If you are not welling to read too much and remebre stuff then don't take this class,I wish someone told me that before

Friendly and interesting prof, very knowledgeable. Sometimes lectures are a bit slow and boring, but they're interesting for the most part if you enjoy arch. Exams are straightforward, read the textbook and lecture notes. If you miss class, its easy to catch up, he posts lecture slides online.

Really nice prof but dry lectures. Don't need to attend them because he has lecture recordings (at least this semester he does). Exams are not tricky, very straight forward. As long as you read the textbook and lecture notes you will be fine!
